extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is violated. rec_drug_a can only become true via the single rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is the conjunction of eight booleans including renal_impairment. Thus any input that makes rec_drug_a true must have renal_impairment = true. But renal_impairment = true also fires rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which sets rec_drug_b := true. No other rule writes rec_drug_b, so nothing can reset it to false (the default applies only to outputs left unset, and rec_drug_b is set by the trigger). Therefore rec_drug_a true logically implies rec_drug_b true, and the two can never be mutually exclusive whenever rec_drug_a fires. Counterexample: set all eight conjuncts (renal_impairment, g6pd_deficiency, heart_failure, immunosuppressed, seizure_history, qt_prolongation, peptic_ulcer, bradycardia) to true; remaining inputs (anticoagulated, elderly, pregnancy) are irrelevant to a/b and set false. Then rec_a_from_conjunction makes rec_drug_a = true and rec_b_from_trigger makes rec_drug_b = true, so both are jointly true and the property is violated.
